title: AccessFlow 23 – Workplace Access & Resource Request Management System Frontend

requirements.summary: Apply professional enterprise visual theme with deep navy/slate primary palette, teal/cyan accents, card-based layouts, status color coding, and collapsible left sidebar navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Consistent color palette and typography applied across all pages
Collapsible left sidebar with navigation links to all modules
Status badges use the defined color coding system
Layout is usable on mobile screen widths

file_operations.description: Extend the Tailwind theme to include enterprise color palette: deep navy/slate primary colors (e.g., navy-900, slate-800), teal/cyan accent colors (teal-500, cyan-400), and status colors (yellow for Submitted, blue for Approved, green for Completed, red for Rejected, orange for In Fulfillment). Configure the Inter font family as the default sans-serif.

Add global CSS custom properties for enterprise theme colors, define base styles for card shadows and transitions, and configure default body/html styling with the Inter font stack. Include utility classes for status badges with the defined color coding.

Create a responsive Layout component with a collapsible left sidebar navigation and top navigation bar. The sidebar includes navigation links to Dashboard, Create Request, My Requests, Approvals (role-conditional), Reports (admin-only), and Admin Config (admin-only). Include mobile hamburger menu toggle and ensure the layout is mobile-friendly.

requirements.summary: Implement Login Page with company-branded sign-in form, Internet Identity login button, MFA indicator badge, and role selector for demo RBAC personas
acceptanceCriteria: Login form renders with email, password fields and a sign-in button
Role selector allows switching between Employee, Manager, IT Admin, Finance Admin
MFA badge/indicator is visible on the page
Successful login navigates to the Dashboard

file_operations.description: Create a Login Page with a centered card containing the company logo (use generated image frontend/public/assets/generated/accessflow-logo.dim_128x128.png), email and password input fields (non-functional demo fields), an Internet Identity login button that triggers useInternetIdentity().login(), an MFA indicator badge, and a role selector dropdown for demo purposes (Employee, Manager, IT Admin, Finance Admin). Use the generated background image frontend/public/assets/generated/login-bg.dim_1440x900.png as the page background. On successful login, navigate to the Dashboard using React Router. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Create Dashboard page with KPI summary cards, recent requests table with SLA countdown, requests-by-status donut chart, and requests-by-category bar chart
acceptanceCriteria: Four KPI cards display with correct labels and mock/live data
Recent requests table renders with status badges and SLA countdown timers
Donut chart shows request distribution by status
Bar chart shows request distribution by category
Role-specific action buttons differ between Employee and Admin views

file_operations.description: Create a Dashboard page with four KPI cards (Total Requests, Pending Approvals, Completed This Month, SLA Breaches), a recent requests table showing Request ID, Type, Status badge with color coding, Submitted Date, and SLA countdown timer, a donut chart for requests by status, and a bar chart for requests by category. Fetch data from backend via listAllRequests() or getRequestsByUser(). Adapt visible actions and filters based on the logged-in user's role using getCallerUserRole(). Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a custom hook useRequests that wraps React Query to fetch all requests using listAllRequests() for admins or getRequestsByUser() for regular users. Include hooks for fetching a single request (getRequest) and submitting new requests (createRequest). Integrate with useActor to access the backend actor.
Create a reusable KPICard component that displays a metric label, value, and optional trend indicator. Apply card-based styling with subtle shadows as defined in the theme.

Create a StatusBadge component that displays a colored badge based on request status using the theme's status color coding: yellow for Submitted/Pending, blue for Approved, green for Completed, red for Rejected, orange for In Fulfillment/In Progress.
Create an SLACountdown component that takes a deadline timestamp and displays a live countdown timer. Highlight warnings when the SLA is at risk or breached.

requirements.summary: Build Create Request page with a 3-step wizard: type selection, details form, and AI pre-submission analysis panel
acceptanceCriteria: Three-step wizard with progress indicator renders correctly
Step 1 shows four request type cards with icons
Step 2 form captures all required fields including justification and priority
Step 3 displays AI analysis panel with simulated suggestions and warnings
Submitting the form creates a new request record in the backend
User is redirected to the request detail page after submission

file_operations.description: Create a Create Request page with a 3-step wizard: Step 1 displays four request type cards (System Access, Physical Access, Equipment, Software Licenses) with icons; Step 2 shows a form for sub-category, justification text area, priority selector, cost estimate input, and document attachment field (non-functional); Step 3 displays an AI Pre-Submission Analysis panel with simulated duplicate access warnings, missing info alerts, suggested access level, and recommended approval chain using rule-based frontend logic. Include a progress indicator showing which step is active. On submit, call createRequest() from the backend and navigate to the request detail page. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create a RequestTypeCard component that displays an icon, title, and description for each request type. Make the card clickable to select a request type in Step 1 of the wizard.

Create an AIAnalysisPanel component that displays simulated AI analysis results including duplicate access warnings, missing information alerts, suggested access level, and recommended approval chain. Use rule-based logic to simulate AI analysis based on form inputs (e.g., high-risk categories trigger multi-level approval warnings).

requirements.summary: Build Request Detail Page with full metadata, approval chain timeline, status progress stepper, SLA countdown, AI risk flags panel, and audit log feed
acceptanceCriteria: All request metadata fields are displayed
Approval chain renders as a vertical timeline with approver names and statuses
Status stepper highlights the current stage
SLA countdown timer counts down from the deadline
AI risk flags panel shows warning messages when applicable
Audit log lists all actions taken on the request with timestamps

file_operations.description: Create a Request Detail Page that fetches a single request using getRequest(requestId) and displays all metadata (type, category, status, priority, cost, justification). Include an approval chain timeline showing each approver node with their role, status, and timestamp; a status progress stepper (Submitted → Under Review → Approved → In Fulfillment → Completed) highlighting the current stage; an SLA countdown timer using the SLACountdown component; an AI risk flags panel displaying simulated warnings; and an activity/audit log feed at the bottom fetched via getAuditLogsForRequest(requestId). Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create an ApprovalTimeline component that renders a vertical timeline of approval steps. Each step displays the approver role, approval status, timestamp, and comments. Use the enterprise theme colors for status indicators.

Create a StatusStepper component that displays a horizontal or vertical stepper showing the request lifecycle stages (Submitted, Under Review, Approved, In Fulfillment, Completed). Highlight the current stage based on the request status.

Create an AIRiskPanel component that displays simulated AI-generated risk warnings and compliance alerts (e.g., 'Warning: user already has admin access in 3 systems'). Use rule-based frontend logic to generate warnings based on request data.
Create an AuditLogFeed component that displays a list of audit log entries with action, actor, timestamp, and notes. Fetch audit logs using getAuditLogsForRequest().

requirements.summary: Build Approvals Screen listing pending requests with action buttons (Approve, Reject, Request More Info) for Manager, IT Admin, and Finance Admin roles
acceptanceCriteria: Approvals list page renders with pending requests filtered by logged-in approver role
Risk level badge is shown per request row
Approve and Reject buttons update request status in the backend
Auto-approval logic sets low-risk software requests to Approved without manual action
High-risk requests display multi-level approval chain
Audit log entry is created on each approval/rejection action

file_operations.description: Create an Approvals Screen that lists all pending requests requiring the logged-in user's approval. Fetch requests using listAllRequests() and filter by current approver. Display requester name, request type, risk level badge, cost, and action buttons (Approve, Reject, Request More Info). On Approve, call approveRequest(requestId, comments); on Reject, call rejectRequest(requestId, comments); on Request More Info, call requestMoreInfo(requestId, comments). Show a note about auto-approval logic for low-risk software requests. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create an ApprovalActionButtons component that renders Approve, Reject, and Request More Info buttons. Include a comments modal for approvers to provide notes before taking action.

requirements.summary: Build AI Chatbot Assistant panel with natural language input, NLP parsing, structured request summary, and one-click request creation
acceptanceCriteria: Floating chatbot button is visible on all pages
Chatbot sidebar opens on click and accepts text input
Entering a natural language request displays a structured parsed output (category, priority, quantity, reason)
A 'Create This Request' button pre-fills the Create Request wizard
Suggested example prompts are shown when the chat is empty

file_operations.description: Create a ChatbotAssistant component with a floating button visible on all pages that expands to a sidebar chat panel. The chatbot accepts natural language input and uses rule-based frontend logic to parse the input into structured fields (category, priority, quantity, reason). Display the parsed request summary and include a 'Create This Request' button that navigates to the Create Request page with pre-filled form data using URL params or state. Show example prompt suggestions when the chat is empty (e.g., 'I need access to CRM for a marketing campaign', 'Need laptop urgently for new joiner tomorrow').

requirements.summary: Build Reports & Export page with filters, summary statistics charts, export buttons (CSV, PDF), department breakdown table, and audit trail log table
acceptanceCriteria: Reports page is accessible only to Admin/Management roles
Filter bar updates all charts and tables on apply
Line chart shows request volume over time
CSV export downloads a correctly formatted file with request data
PDF print view renders a clean summary layout
Department breakdown table groups requests by department with counts and SLA compliance %
Audit trail table supports text search and pagination

file_operations.description: Create a Reports & Export page accessible only to Admin/Management roles (guard using getCallerUserRole()). Include a filter bar for date range, department, and request type. Display summary statistics with charts: a line chart showing request volume over time, a pie chart for approval rate, and a bar chart for SLA compliance. Add export buttons for CSV download (using client-side CSV generation from listAllRequests() data) and a print-friendly PDF summary view. Include a department-wise breakdown table grouping requests by department with counts and SLA compliance percentages. Add an audit trail log table with search and pagination fetched via listAuditLogs(). Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create a ReportFilters component with date range picker, department dropdown, and request type multi-select. Emit filter change events to update charts and tables.

Create utility functions for exporting data to CSV format and generating a print-friendly HTML view for PDF summary. Use client-side libraries (e.g., csv-parse or manual CSV generation) to create downloadable CSV files from request data.

requirements.summary: Build Admin Configuration page with tabs for User Management, SLA Rules, Approval Chain Policies, and System Notifications
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Config page renders with four tabs
User Management tab lists all users and allows role and delegate assignment
SLA Rules tab allows editing deadline hours per request category
Approval Chain Policies tab shows and allows editing of routing rules per risk level
All changes are persisted to backend stable storage

file_operations.description: Create an Admin Configuration page accessible only to Admin roles (guard using getCallerUserRole()). Include four tabs: (1) User Management — list users via listUsers(), allow role assignment using assignUserRole(), and set manager delegates via setManagerDelegate(); (2) SLA Rules — list and edit SLA timelines per category using listSlaRules() and setSlaRule(); (3) Approval Chain Policies — list and edit approval routing rules using listApprovalPolicies() and addApprovalPolicy(); (4) System Notifications — toggle in-app notification preferences using setNotificationPreference(). All changes must persist to backend. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a UserManagementTab component that lists all users in a table with columns for name, role, department, and manager delegate. Include inline edit controls for role assignment and delegate selection. Call assignUserRole() and setManagerDelegate() to persist changes.
Create an SLARulesTab component that lists SLA rules per request category with editable deadline fields. Call setSlaRule() to save changes.
Create an ApprovalPoliciesTab component that lists approval chain policies per request type and risk level. Allow editing of required roles and add new policies via addApprovalPolicy().
Create a NotificationsTab component that allows admins to toggle in-app notification preferences for users via setNotificationPreference().

requirements.summary: Implement in-app notification system with bell icon, unread count badge, notification dropdown, and mark-as-read functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Bell icon in navbar shows unread count badge
Clicking bell opens a dropdown with notification list
Each notification shows message text and timestamp
Mark as read updates the notification in the backend and removes the badge count

file_operations.description: Create a NotificationBell component that displays a bell icon in the top navigation bar with an unread count badge. Fetch notifications using getNotifications(userId) and unread count using getUnreadNotificationCount(userId). On click, open a dropdown listing notification messages with timestamps. Include a 'Mark as read' button for each notification that calls markNotificationAsRead(userId, notificationId). Integrate with useActor and useInternetIdentity to get the current user's principal. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a custom hook useNotifications that wraps React Query to fetch notifications and unread count for the current user. Include mutations for marking notifications as read. Integrate with useActor.
